items.title: Geometry-aware similarity metrics for neural representations on Riemannian and statistical manifolds
items.authors: N Alex Cayco Gajic, Arthur Pellegrino
items.abstract: Similarity measures are widely used to interpret the representational geometries used by neural networks to solve tasks. Yet, because existing methods compare the extrinsic geometry of representations in state space, rather than their intrinsic geometry, they may fail to capture subtle yet crucial d...

items.title: On-the-fly Repulsion in the Contextual Space for Rich Diversity in Diffusion Transformers
items.authors: Omer Dahary, Benaya Koren, Daniel Garibi et al.
items.abstract: Modern Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved remarkable semantic alignment, yet they often suffer from a significant lack of variety, converging on a narrow set of visual solutions for any given prompt. This typicality bias presents a challenge for creative applications that require a w...

items.title: Adaptive Block-Scaled Data Types
items.authors: Jack Cook, Hyemin S. Lee, Kathryn Le et al.
items.abstract: NVFP4 has grown increasingly popular as a 4-bit format for quantizing large language models due to its hardware support and its ability to retain useful information with relatively few bits per parameter. However, the format is not without limitations: recent work has shown that NVFP4 suffers from i...

items.title: ParaSpeechCLAP: A Dual-Encoder Speech-Text Model for Rich Stylistic Language-Audio Pretraining
items.authors: Anuj Diwan, Eunsol Choi, David Harwath
items.abstract: We introduce ParaSpeechCLAP, a dual-encoder contrastive model that maps speech and text style captions into a common embedding space, supporting a wide range of intrinsic (speaker-level) and situational (utterance-level) descriptors (such as pitch, texture and emotion) far beyond the narrow set hand...

items.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation
items.authors: Kaituo Feng, Manyuan Zhang, Shuang Chen et al.
items.abstract: Recent image generation models have shown strong capabilities in generating high-fidelity and photorealistic images. However, they are fundamentally constrained by frozen internal knowledge, thus often failing on real-world scenarios that are knowledge-intensive or require up-to-date information. In...

items.title: HandX: Scaling Bimanual Motion and Interaction Generation
items.authors: Zimu Zhang, Yucheng Zhang, Xiyan Xu et al.
items.abstract: Synthesizing human motion has advanced rapidly, yet realistic hand motion and bimanual interaction remain underexplored. Whole-body models often miss the fine-grained cues that drive dexterous behavior, finger articulation, contact timing, and inter-hand coordination, and existing resources lack hig...

items.title: SHOW3D: Capturing Scenes of 3D Hands and Objects in the Wild
items.authors: Patrick Rim, Kevin Harris, Braden Copple et al.
items.abstract: Accurate 3D understanding of human hands and objects during manipulation remains a significant challenge for egocentric computer vision. Existing hand-object interaction datasets are predominantly captured in controlled studio settings, which limits both environmental diversity and the ability of mo...

items.title: FocusVLA: Focused Visual Utilization for Vision-Language-Action Models
items.authors: Yichi Zhang, Weihao Yuan, Yizhuo Zhang et al.
items.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models improve action generation by conditioning policies on rich vision-language information. However, current auto-regressive policies are constrained by three bottlenecks: (1) architectural bias drives models to overlook visual details, (2) an excessive number of visu...
